layout: lifecycle (Option B): inputs/ → skills/ → outputs/ + misc/
description: AI-agent-facing index of every file in the SYNTHESIS folder. Use 'topical_index' for query routing; use 'files' for per-file metadata. lifecycle_stage values: source | tool | generated_output | orphan | meta.
fictional_dataset_warning: All NBFG content is a fictional bank used for demos. Do not treat any figure, person, or claim as real-world fact.

agent_routing_hints.first_question_to_ask: Is the user asking about (a) NBFG content/data, (b) running an analytics skill, or (c) reading already-generated skill outputs?
agent_routing_hints.for_b_use: files where lifecycle_stage='tool' (start with the matching SKILL.md under skills/)
agent_routing_hints.for_c_use: files where lifecycle_stage='generated_output' AND is_latest=true (everything in outputs/latest/)

files.summary: Folder map and how-to-use guide for SYNTHESIS. Single entry point for new readers.
example_queries: What's in the SYNTHESIS folder?
Where do I start?

files.summary: Review of the SYNTHESIS folder structure with reorganization options. Captures the rationale for the current Option B layout.
example_queries: Why is the folder organized this way?

files.summary: This file. Machine-readable per-file index plus topical query routing for AI agents.
example_queries: Which file should I read for X?

files.summary: Schema reference for the eight workforce CSVs in inputs/nbfg/data/. Includes LOB/level/region/currency code tables and per-file column docs.
example_queries: What does L4 mean?
What are the LOB codes?
What columns are in the engagement survey CSV?

files.owner: Office of the CEO (Margaret L. Chen)
files.summary: Northbridge Financial Group three-year enterprise strategy. Five pillars including 'Be the Employer of Choice in North American Banking'. 50,000 employees, $4.3B 2024 NIBT, 12.8% CET1.
example_queries: What is NBFG's enterprise strategy?
What are the five strategic pillars?
What is the 2024 NIBT baseline?
Who is the CEO of Northbridge?

files.owner: James Whitford, Group Head Wealth Management
files.summary: Wealth Management 2025 strategy. Talent-led growth: +5% net advisor headcount, +12% AUM YoY, $385B AUM by 2027, $24M incremental talent investment.
example_queries: What is the Wealth Management strategy?
How many advisors did Wealth plan to hire?
What's the AUM target?
What is Project Compass?

files.summary: Technology 2025 strategy. 6,000 engineers, 16% 2024 attrition, Toronto senior eng comp 12-15% below market median per McLean & Co. study, $2.4B 2025-2027 envelope.
example_queries: What is the Technology strategy?
Why is Toronto Tech attrition so high?
What did the McLean & Co. comp study find?
How much is NBFG investing in Technology?

files.summary: People & Culture 2025 strategy. 71 enterprise engagement, 11.3% voluntary attrition (up from 9.9%), 6.4% regrettable attrition (above 5.5% target). Three pressure points: Toronto Tech, Capital Markets, Wealth advisors.
example_queries: What is the People & Culture strategy?
What is the regrettable attrition target?
Where are the engagement pressure points?
Who is the CHRO?

files.summary: Board-level enterprise performance review for full-year 2025. $4.6B NIBT (+7% YoY), 14.3% ROE, 56.4% efficiency ratio, 12.6% CET1, 12.4% voluntary attrition, 70 engagement.
example_queries: What were 2025 enterprise results?
Did NBFG hit its 2025 financial plan?
What is the 2025 ROE?
How did engagement and attrition end the year?

files.owner: Sebastian R. Holm, Group Head Capital Markets
files.summary: Capital Markets 2025 review. Record financials: $820M NIBT (+18%), 22% ROE. Engagement at enterprise low of 52. ~7% gender comp gap at VP/L6 under remediation.
example_queries: How did Capital Markets perform in 2025?
Why is Capital Markets engagement so low?
Is there a pay equity issue in Capital Markets?
What is FICC revenue?

files.owner: Marie-Claire Tremblay, Group Head Canadian P&C
files.summary: Canadian P&C 2025 review. $1.8B NIBT (+3%), $5.2B revenue (+1%), 53.0% efficiency, 78 engagement (highest revenue-generating LOB), 180K net new customers.
example_queries: How did Canadian P&C perform in 2025?
Which LOB has the highest engagement?
How many new customers did P&C add?

files.summary: Wealth Management Q4 2025 plan-vs-actual. Behind plan on every metric: 0% advisor growth (target +5%), 14% regrettable attrition (worse than 12% prior), $590M NIBT (-3% YoY vs $695M plan).
example_queries: Did Wealth hit its 2025 plan?
What is the Wealth strategy execution gap?
How many advisors did Wealth actually hire vs plan?

files.summary: 2025 enterprise engagement survey executive summary. 70 enterprise score (-1 vs 2024), 82% participation, 41,000 respondents. Top vs bottom LOB gap: 28pts (widest on record). Toronto Tech compensation perception 50/100 vs 64 enterprise.
example_queries: What did the 2025 engagement survey find?
What's the engagement score by LOB?
Which LOB scored lowest on engagement?

files.summary: 2025 compensation philosophy. Five principles: pay-for-performance, market-competitive (±5% of median), shareholder alignment, equity, transparency. Pay-mix table by level. LTI in PSUs/RSUs with clawback.
example_queries: What is NBFG's compensation philosophy?
How does pay mix change by level?
What are the comp principles?
What is the LTI structure?

files.summary: CEO year-end letter, December 2025. $4.6B NIBT (+7%), 14.3% ROE, Capital Markets record year, US franchise 3rd consecutive year of efficiency improvement, Wealth shortfall acknowledged. Variable comp pool funded at 105% of target.
example_queries: What did the CEO say about 2025?
Did NBFG meet plan in 2025?
What was the variable comp funding decision?

files.scope: month x LOB x region x city x level
files.summary: Monthly attrition by LOB/region/city/level. Includes voluntary/involuntary/regrettable splits and top_reason free-text.
example_queries: What is Toronto Technology attrition?
Which LOBs have rising attrition?
What's the top reason people are leaving?

files.scope: year x LOB x region x level x gender
files.summary: Annual compensation summary by LOB/region/level/gender. Includes mean and median base + total comp; currency column flags FX-normalization need.
example_queries: What is the gender pay gap by level?
How does compensation compare across regions?
What's the average total comp at L6?

files.scope: year x LOB x region x level x gender x ethnicity
files.summary: Annual diversity demographics: headcount by gender x ethnicity_group across LOB/region/level.
example_queries: What is the gender breakdown by level?
How is diversity changing year over year?

files.scope: year x LOB x region x dimension
files.summary: Engagement survey results by LOB/region/dimension. Includes external benchmark and participation rate. Dimensions include Overall, Compensation & Rewards, Career Growth, Wellbeing, Manager Effectiveness, etc.
example_queries: What is the engagement score for Capital Markets?
How does Toronto Tech compensation perception compare to enterprise?
Which dimension scores worst?

files.scope: quarter x LOB x region x city x level
files.summary: Quarterly headcount by LOB/region/city/level. The largest CSV; the master headcount table.
example_queries: How many people work in Wealth?
What is Toronto Tech headcount trend?
How big is Capital Markets?

files.scope: month x LOB x region x city x level
files.summary: Monthly hires by LOB/region/city/level. Includes source (e.g. external/referral) and time-to-fill.
example_queries: How many advisors did Wealth hire in 2025?
What is time-to-fill for senior engineering roles?
Where are most hires sourced?

files.scope: quarter x from_LOB x to_LOB x level
files.summary: Internal movement counts: people moving from one LOB to another, by quarter and level.
example_queries: Where do people move when they leave Technology?
What is internal mobility into Wealth?

files.scope: year x LOB x level x gender
files.summary: Annual promotion rates by LOB/level/gender. Includes eligible_count, promoted_count, and computed rate.
example_queries: What is the promotion rate for women at L4?
Are men and women promoted at the same rate?
Which LOB has the highest promotion rate?

files.summary: Skill that cross-references strategy documents (Word/PDF/PowerPoint) against workforce CSVs to detect strategy/execution gaps. Outputs a 1-page PDF brief and a ~5-tab Excel workbook.
example_queries: Run a strategic alignment check
Where is strategy diverging from execution?

files.summary: Skill that computes gender comp gaps at LOB+level grain and detects regional comp compression after FX-normalization. Outputs a 1-page PDF brief and a ~7-tab Excel workbook.
example_queries: Run a pay equity review
Where are the gender pay gaps?

files.summary: Skill that builds a composite risk score per LOB+region from engagement, attrition, hiring, and compensation signals. Outputs a 1-page PDF brief and a ~6-tab Excel workbook.
example_queries: Find the workforce risk hotspots
Where are engagement and attrition converging?

files.summary: Bash script that copies the three NBFG demo skills into ~/.claude/skills/ (or a custom path).
example_queries: How do I install these skills?

files.summary: Helper module imported by each skill: locates input CSVs in the target folder. Default path lookup tries inputs/nbfg/data/ first then falls back to legacy NBFG_Demo_Pack/data/.
Helper module imported by each skill: writes timestamped briefs and workbooks with BMO Corporate Classic styling.
Core analysis logic for strategic-alignment-check: parses strategy docs, infers LOB intent (growth/maintain/reduce), reconciles against workforce data.
Output builder for strategic-alignment-check: writes the 1-page PDF brief and the multi-tab Excel detail workbook.
Validator/smoke-test for strategic-alignment-check: checks that required inputs are present and the pipeline runs end-to-end.
Core analysis logic for pay-equity-review: computes signed gender comp gaps and FX-normalized regional compression.
Output builder for pay-equity-review: writes the 1-page PDF brief and the ~7-tab Excel workbook.
Validator/smoke-test for pay-equity-review.
Core analysis logic for risk-hot-spots: composite scoring across attrition, engagement, hiring, and compensation signals.
Output builder for risk-hot-spots: writes the 1-page PDF brief and the ~6-tab Excel workbook.
Validator/smoke-test for risk-hot-spots.
Canonical latest 1-page strategic alignment brief — top-5 strategy/execution gaps with severity coding.
Canonical latest strategic alignment detail workbook (~5 tabs: Summary, Findings, Documents Reviewed, LOB Trend Metrics, Methodology).
Canonical latest pay equity 1-page brief — top-5 findings ranked by gap magnitude and population affected.
Canonical latest pay equity detail workbook (~7 tabs: Summary, Findings, Gap by LOB+Level (Gender), Gap by LOB (Gender), Regional Compression, Detail by LOB+Level+Region, Methodology).
Canonical latest risk hotspots 1-page brief — top-5 hotspots with composite scores and drivers.
Canonical latest risk hotspots detail workbook (~6 tabs: Summary, Findings, LOB+Region Risk Inputs, City Attrition Detail, Engagement Summary, Methodology).

Archived pay equity detail (17:58 run).

Archived pay equity detail (first run, 17:56).

Explains why files in misc/ are there. Currently documents the orphaned vantrax_submission_log.csv.
example_queries: What's in the misc folder?
What is vantrax?

files.summary: Small CSV (20 rows) listing employee_id, name, reference_number, status. All rows have status='submitted'. Not connected to NBFG demo. See misc/NOTE.md.
example_queries: What is vantrax?
Who submitted PRV-2026 references?

changelog.summary: Reorganized SYNTHESIS into Option B layout (inputs/skills/outputs/misc). All paths updated. Added README.md, DATA_DICTIONARY.md, misc/NOTE.md. Created outputs/latest/ canonical copies (no timestamps); moved historical runs to outputs/archive/{skill}/. Removed __pycache__/. Resolved the duplicate _skill_outputs/ folders. Updated skill SKILL.md and data_discovery.py default paths.
